data.title: Paramount Global cut to hold at Deutsche Bank on ad outlook
data.description:
Paramount Global (NASDAQ:PARA)  was cut to hold from buy at Deutsche Bank on risks to the advertising outlook and more attractive investment alternatives.

``We see the risk/reward as more balanced now, especially in light of what we see as increasing risk to the advertising outlook due to macroeconomic factors," Deutsche Bank analyst Brian Kraft, who also cut the Paramount (NASDAQ:PARA) price target to $12 from $15, wrote in a note. 

Kraft had believed that Paramount (PARA) shares were attractive, with Class B holders entitled to sell half of their shares for $15 in the Skydance deal, though the advertising outlook is now more uncertain due to macroeconomic factors. 

Companies that are in a better position to weather a cyclical downturn include Fox (FOXA) and Formula One (FWONA) or have more attractive valuations, with more clarity into their outlooks  include Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) and Disney (DIS).

Shares of Paramount (PARA) ticked lower by 0.4% on Wednesday.

data.title: Rocket Cos. upgraded to Buy at Deutsche Bank, to Market Perform at KBW
data.description:
Rocket Companies (NYSE:RKT) stock jumped 15% as Deutsche Bank and KBW upgraded the lender's stock after it agreed to acquire Mr. Cooper (COOP) in $9.4B all-stock deal earlier this week.

Deutsche Bank analyst Mark DeVries upgraded RKT to Buy from Hold, expecting the acquisition will "provide a big step toward the ambitious 2027 market share targets laid out last September (at least the 20% refi share target)."

He lifted its price target to $16 from $14. He sees more upside potential in a lower rate environment, where the revenue synergies from boosting Mr. Cooper's recapture "should be more pronounced."

KBW analyst Bose George raised Rocket (NYSE:RKT) to Market Perform from Underperform, as he sees the Mr. Cooper (COOP) deal creating a more balanced business model, providing more confidence in earnings estimates.

"RKT's 2027 market share goals appear more attainable, considering the COOP deal would roughly quadruple RKT's servicing UPB to ~$2.15T and position RKT to utilize its industry-leading recapture capabilities in any declining rate environment," George wrote in a note to clients.

The recommendations of Hold or better from Deutsche Bank and KBW contrast with the SA Quant rating of Sell. The average SA Analyst rating  is Buy, agreeing with the Deutsche Bank recommendation, while the average Wall Street rating  of Hold aligns with KBW's assessment.

data.title: BlackBerry Q4 2025 highlights revenue growth and strategic focus on QNX expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: BlackBerry Limited (BB) Q4 2025

Management View

  • BlackBerry CEO John Giamatteo highlighted the transformative year for the company, noting the successful sale of the Cylance business and a strong finish to the fiscal year. Total company revenue reached $141.7 million, surpassing the top end of guidance. QNX revenue was $65.8 million, and Secure Communications revenue reached $67.3 million, both exceeding expectations. Licensing also performed better than expected at $8.6 million.
  • Giamatteo emphasized the completion of cost reduction actions that exceeded the goal of $150 million in savings, transforming BlackBerry’s profitability. Adjusted EBITDA for the year reached $39.3 million, reflecting a $54 million improvement year-over-year when adjusted for the patent sale in early FY2024.
  • CFO Tim Foote reported that QNX gross margins remained strong at 83% for the quarter and 84% for the full fiscal year. Secure Communications achieved gross margins of 64% for Q4 and 66% for the year, influenced by revenue mix.
  • The company highlighted strategic investments in QNX’s next-generation products, including the SDP 8.0 platform and cloud-based digital cockpit solution, QNX Cabin. These initiatives target automotive and adjacent verticals such as medical and industrial markets, with significant new customer wins reported across regions.
  • BlackBerry announced plans to rename the IoT division to QNX to better reflect the business focus and brand recognition.

Outlook

  • Management provided guidance for fiscal Q1 2026, expecting QNX revenue between $51 million and $55 million and adjusted EBITDA of $2 million to $6 million. Full-year revenue guidance for QNX was set at $250 million to $270 million, with adjusted EBITDA expected to range from $55 million to $60 million.
  • Secure Communications revenue for Q1 2026 is projected to be between $50 million and $54 million, with EBITDA between $3 million and $6 million. Full-year Secure Communications revenue is anticipated in the range of $230 million to $240 million, with adjusted EBITDA between $34 million and $44 million.
  • Total company revenue guidance for FY2026 is set at $504 million to $534 million, with adjusted EBITDA in the range of $69 million to $84 million.

Financial Results

  • BlackBerry reported adjusted EBITDA of $21.1 million for Q4 2025, representing 15% of revenue. Adjusted EPS, including discontinued operations, was $0.03 for the quarter and $0.02 for the full fiscal year, both above expectations.
  • The company’s cash and investments increased by $144 million during the quarter, supported by strong operating cash flow of $42 million and proceeds from the Cylance sale. Total cash and investments now stand at $410 million, with a net cash position exceeding $200 million.
  • For the full fiscal year, BlackBerry achieved total revenue of $534.9 million, with Secure Communications contributing $272.6 million, QNX $236 million, and Licensing $26.3 million.

Q&A

  • Paul Treiber, RBC Capital Markets, inquired about the impact of tariffs on automotive OEMs. CEO Giamatteo stated there had been no significant supply chain issues reported by customers and noted that over 50% of QNX revenue comes from outside the U.S., providing some insulation.
  • Todd Coupland, CIBC, asked about the company’s assumptions regarding tariffs in guidance. CFO Foote explained that the broader range reflects potential uncertainties but maintained confidence in achieving the top-end of guidance.
  • Analysts also questioned the growth potential of QNX beyond automotive. Management emphasized investments in general embedded markets and reported new wins in medical, industrial, and aerospace sectors.
  • Luke Junk, Baird, raised questions on the vehicle OS initiative. Management explained that the initiative could expand the content per vehicle opportunity and strengthen partnerships with OEMs globally.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on guidance uncertainties and macroeconomic factors such as tariffs and government spending. Their tone was neutral to slightly positive.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing strategic initiatives and a strong fiscal year finish. The focus on investments in QNX and diversification of revenue sources was highlighted.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management demonstrated increased confidence, particularly in QNX’s growth potential and streamlined Secure Communications operations.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue for Q4 2025 was slightly lower than Q3 2025, attributed to seasonal variations and revenue mix. However, both quarters exceeded guidance, showing consistent operational strength.
  • Adjusted EBITDA decreased slightly quarter-over-quarter due to investment in QNX and a shift in revenue mix.
  • Management’s tone remained confident, with a stronger focus on QNX’s growth potential compared to the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged uncertainties in the automotive market due to tariff changes and macroeconomic conditions but emphasized revenue diversification across regions and verticals to mitigate risks.
  • Secure Communications faces potential volatility in government spending, but long-term contracts and mission-critical solutions provide stability.
  • Analysts highlighted concerns regarding the timing of tariff impacts and potential delays in adopting QNX’s new offerings.

Final Takeaway

BlackBerry concluded FY2025 on a high note, exceeding guidance across all divisions and demonstrating significant progress in profitability and cash flow. Strategic investments in QNX’s next-generation platforms and expansion into adjacent markets position the company for future growth. Management remains confident in navigating macroeconomic uncertainties while focusing on long-term value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: JFrog shares up, Barclays says DevOps SaaS price raise took place
data.description:
JFrog (NASDAQ:FROG) stock rose nearly 3% in morning trading on Wednesday after Barclays said the software platform increased the price of its DevOps SaaS Enterprise X tier increased about 27%.

The brokerage said, JFrog’s website shows a price increase in its DevOps SaaS Enterprise X tier to $950​/​month from $750​/​month. The company also added an option to purchase additional ML credits at $1.20/credit to its SaaS and Self-Hosted plans.

The stock was trading at $33.38 on Wednesday.

Analysts are bullish on the stock, with Seeking Alpha, Wall Street and Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings considering it a Buy.

The company, which provides a hybrid software supply chain platform, grew 13.5% so far this year, compared to the over 4% decline in the broader S&P500 Index.

data.title: 10 weakest Canadian stocks to watch as investors await Trump’s tariff plan
data.description:
Investors and product producers worldwide are eagerly awaiting key announcements from the U.S. administration on tariffs, on a day President Donald Trump has dubbed "Liberation Day."

While further tariffs are expected on more countries, Canada will continue to get some attention amid an already escalating trade war between it and the U.S. 

In this context, Seeking Alpha highlighted a list of 10 Canadian stocks that may face heightened risks. These companies, identified as having the weakest Seeking Alpha quant metrics, are already underperforming and could experience even greater declines if additional negative news surfaces. With such precarious positions, these stocks are particularly susceptible to potential further downward pressure as the tariff situation evolves. 

Worst Rated Canadian Stocks Per SA Quant Grades

No. 10: Lundin Mining Corporation (OTCPK:LUNMF), quant grade of 2.79. 

No. 9: Teck Resources Limited (NYSE:TECK), quant grade of 2.79. 

No. 8: Rogers Communications Inc. (NYSE:RCI), quant grade of 2.78. 

No. 7: Cameco Corporation (NYSE:CCJ), quant grade of 2.73. 

No. 6: Sprott (NYSE:SII), quant grade of 2.45. 

No. 5: TransAlta Corporation (NYSE:TAC), quant grade of 2.32. 

No. 4: North American Construction Group (NYSE:NOA), quant grade of 2.04. 

No. 3: VersaBank (NASDAQ:VBNK), quant grade of 1.58. 

No. 2: TFI International (NYSE:TFII), quant grade of 1.35. 

No. 1: BRP Inc (NASDAQ:DOOO), quant grade of 1.31. 

Year-to-date price action: LUNMF -3.9%, TECK -8.7%, RCI -17.1%, CCJ -18.8%, SII +5.7%, TAC -33.5%, NOA -26.6%, VBNK -27.1%, TFII -42.5%, and DOOO -29.5%. 

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

Canada ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:FXC), (NYSEARCA:FLCA), and (BATS:BBCA).

data.title: BHP considered spinning off iron ore, coal units in radical pivot - Reuters
data.description:
BHP (NYSE:BHP) considered spinning off its Australian iron ore and coal divisions in 2023-24 as part of a medium-term growth strategy, Reuters reported Wednesday.

BHP (NYSE:BHP) would keep its South Australian copper assets in such a move, but with iron ore accounting for ~60% of its profits, it would radically reshape the company after more than half a century of iron ore mining in Australia; unbundling coal with iron ore also would remove most of the company's carbon exposure.

BHP (NYSE:BHP) believed a spinoff of iron ore and coal would generate cash and franking credits that benefit Australian taxpayers, meaning there could be considerable Australian interest in any flotation, and a freed-up copper and potash unit would have more scope to seek out fresh combinations, such as with Teck Resources, the report said.

But the plan was complicated by BHP's failure to purchase Anglo American, which would have bulked up the copper business and helped with cash flow, while the incentive to make its business greener has become less strong as many companies have stepped back from environmental goals.

Ultimately, BHP (BHP) decided the timing was not right for the spinoff plan because the company still required the huge amounts of cash generated by the two Australian divisions to fund capital spending at its Escondida copper complex in Chile and its Jansen potash development in Canada, according to the report.

data.title: Tesla's deliveries report is called a 'disaster' by Wedbush as sell-side confidence fades
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) fell on Wednesday after posting its worst quarterly deliveries number since 2022. Expectations for Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) Q1 earnings report had already been dramatically reduced, but the tally of just 336K deliveries for the quarter was still below the consensus estimate.

In general, Wall Street analysts are bracing for more negative news, with very few "buy the dip" recommendations out there.

RBC analyst Tom Narayan advised that the weak quarter was not a total surprise and already baked in to Tesla's (TSLA) share price to a degree already. "What we might have gotten wrong was expecting a boost to March numbers given a pull forward expectation in the US ahead of potential tariffs," he noted.

Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives said the numbers were a "disaster" on every metric. "Musk needs to stop this political firestorm and balance being CEO of Tesla with DOGE," warned Ives on the self-inflicted crisis. 

Hargreaves Lansdown analyst Matt Britzman said there was no way to sugarcoat the report. "Tesla’s first-quarter delivery numbers are a disappointment, though many investors were already preparing for a soft number. A drop from last year is no surprise, but the scale is worse than many had expected," he wrote. 

Meanwhile, Barclays warned that the soft Q1 deliveries print from Tesla (TSLA) creates a challenging path ahead. For its part, Oppenheimer said it will remain cautious on Tesla (TSLA) after digesting the big decline in deliveries.

Looking ahead, Future Fund Active ETF co-founder and Tesla (TSLA) shareholder Gary Black highlighted that the results will push analysts to reduce full-year 2025 delivery estimates and earnings estimates. Of course, that would also impact the trading multiple, which is already considered elevated by many analysts, even after factoring in the Cybercab, Optimus humanoid robot, FSD licensing, and various AI initiatives.

On Seeking Alpha, analyst Kevin Shan warned on Wednesday that robotaxis are not the answer for Tesla's (TSLA) long-term challenges. "Self-driving and robotics no doubt have potential, but it's a market where Tesla is competing against massive tech companies with healthier core businesses and has already begun generating revenue from their developments," he wrote.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) were down 2.1% in morning trading on Wednesday to $262.80.

data.title: Raymond James revises airline ratings amid market uncertainty, upgrades Allegiant
data.description:
Ahead of quarterly results from the major U.S. carriers, Raymond James adjusted their outlook on certain carriers to reflect the elevated macro uncertainty in the airline industry, including weakness in off-peak leisure travel and the loss of momentum for corporate demand.

Among those singled out is United Airlines Holdings (NASDAQ:UAL) which was downgraded to Market Perform from Outperform. While Raymond James analyst Savanthi Syth sees the recent pullback in United (NASDAQ:UAL) stock as an overcorrection relative to the current environment where premium and long-haul international demand remain solid, United (NASDAQ:UAL) faces greater earnings risk should these segments start to falter. Instead, Syth prefers Delta Air Lines (DAL), which she rates as a Strong Buy.

Syth also gives high marks to Allegiant Travel Company (ALGT) which she upgrades to Strong Buy from Outperform given Allegiant’s (ALGT) "idiosyncratic earnings drivers" that should help offset any macro-driven headwinds. This also applies to Outperform-rated American Airlines Group (AAL), and Southwest Airlines (LUV).

While she sees better risk-reward elsewhere, Syth believes JetBlue Airways (JBLU) offers an “interesting opportunity for those looking to be nimble in the near-term due to extreme bearishness present in the stock.” Syth views JetBlue (JBLU) as a Market Perform airline stock.

After a string of losses reaching as many as six consecutive days in the red, airline stocks are modestly higher with the exception of Allegiant Travel Company (ALGT) up 6% on Tuesday’s upgrade.

In related news, United Airlines (UAL) becomes the first U.S. carrier to offer flights to Bangkok, Thailand, and Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam with daily service beginning in October from Hong Kong.

Also beginning in October, United (UAL) will add a second daily flight between San Francisco and Manila, making the carrier the only U.S. airline to serve the Philippines. The carrier is also adding non-stop flights between the U.S. and Adelaide, Australia, complementing flights to Sydney, Brisbane, and Melbourne.

data.title: AngioDynamics targets 14%-16% MedTech growth for fiscal 2025 driven by thrombectomy and NanoKnife adoption
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: AngioDynamics (ANGO) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jim Clemmer highlighted strong Q3 performance with total revenue reaching $72 million, reflecting over 9% year-over-year growth. MedTech revenue grew by 22%, with continued success across platforms.
  • Clemmer emphasized the performance of the Auryon platform, which grew 17% year-over-year, and the Mechanical Thrombectomy portfolio, including AlphaVac and AngioVac, which combined grew 47%. AlphaVac alone achieved 160% growth.
  • NanoKnife adoption was bolstered by its 16% increase in disposable revenue. The FDA’s expanded indication for NanoKnife in prostate tissue ablation, combined with new CPT codes effective January 2026, positions the product for long-term growth.
  • CFO Stephen Trowbridge noted Q3 adjusted EBITDA of $1.3 million, a turnaround from a loss in the same quarter of 2024, and gross margins improved to 54%.

Outlook

  • Fiscal 2025 revenue guidance was raised to $285-$288 million, with MedTech growth expected in the 14%-16% range.
  • Gross margin guidance increased to 53%-54%, up from the previous 52%-53% estimate.
  • Adjusted EBITDA guidance was raised to $4-$5 million, and adjusted loss per share is expected to range from $0.31 to $0.34.
  • Management expects continued sequential and year-over-year growth in thrombectomy and NanoKnife adoption, particularly as they expand reimbursement and coverage for prostate indications.

Financial Results

  • MedTech revenue reached $31.3 million, contributing 44% of total revenue, compared to 39% in Q3 2024.
  • Auryon revenue was $13.9 million, achieving double-digit growth for the 15th consecutive quarter.
  • AlphaVac revenue grew to $3 million, a 161% year-over-year increase, while AngioVac revenue reached $6.8 million, up 23%.
  • NanoKnife revenue totaled $6.3 million, supported by a 16% rise in probe revenue.
  • The Med Device segment generated $40.7 million, achieving a 1% year-over-year increase.
  • Cash and cash equivalents stood at $44.8 million as of February 28, 2025. The company expects cash generation in Q4, ending the year with approximately $55 million.

Q&A

  • John Young, Canaccord Genuity: Asked about the sustainability of AngioVac’s performance. CFO Trowbridge confirmed that AngioVac’s Q3 performance of $6.8 million is a solid baseline and expects continued growth driven by the synergistic portfolio of AngioVac and AlphaVac.
  • Eduardo Martinez, H.C. Wainwright: Inquired about the AMBITION BTK trial for Auryon. Trowbridge stated the trial will evaluate outcomes for treating critical limb ischemia and aims to demonstrate Auryon’s efficacy in below-the-knee procedures.
  • Steven Lichtman, Oppenheimer: Asked about Auryon’s European market opportunities. CEO Clemmer noted extensive education and awareness campaigns, expecting revenue contributions from Q4.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiment about growth drivers like Mechanical Thrombectomy and NanoKnife, while seeking clarity on market penetration and reimbursement timelines.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing strong execution and strategic positioning in high-growth markets.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts’ tone showed increased optimism about sustained growth in key product segments.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue remained consistent, with Q3 revenue at $72 million compared to Q2’s $73 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA declined slightly from $3.1 million in Q2 to $1.3 million in Q3, attributed to sequential investments.
  • MedTech growth continued to outpace Med Device, with a notable rise in AlphaVac and NanoKnife performance.
  • Guidance was raised for revenue, gross margin, and adjusted EBITDA, reflecting management’s confidence in execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged potential challenges related to reimbursement adoption for NanoKnife’s prostate indications, emphasizing ongoing efforts to secure payer support by the effective date of the new CPT codes.
  • Analysts raised concerns over the pace of adoption in new markets, particularly in Europe for Auryon and AlphaVac.
  • Tariff impacts were noted as potential risks, but management does not expect material effects due to a predominantly U.S.-based supply chain.

Final Takeaway

AngioDynamics delivered strong Q3 results, highlighting growth across its MedTech portfolio. With raised guidance and increasing adoption in key areas like Mechanical Thrombectomy and NanoKnife, the company is positioned for sustained growth. Management’s confidence is underscored by continued investments in clinical data and market expansion, while addressing reimbursement challenges and leveraging synergies across its product lines.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BioMarin succeeds in trial for its metabolic disorder treatment
data.description:
• BioMarin Pharmaceutical (NASDAQ:BMRN) said on Wednesday that its late-stage trial of PALYNZIQ, a treatment for phenylketonuria, met main goal.
  • The study led to a statistically significant lowering in blood Phe levels in adolescents aged 12-17 with phenylketonuria, a metabolic disorder that prevents the body from breaking down the amino acid phenylalanine, compared to diet alone.
  • The company  said that when it came to safety, results were consistent with the known profile of the medicine.
  • Biomarin plans to submit for regulatory approval of PALYNZIQ later this year.
